# Django
DJANGO_SECRET_KEY=change-me-to-a-random-secret-value
DJANGO_DEBUG=true
DJANGO_ALLOWED_HOSTS=localhost 127.0.0.1

# Ollama
OLLAMA_HOST=http://localhost:11434
OLLAMA_MODEL=qwen2.5:7b

FROM python:3.14-slim

ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1
ENV DJANGO_SECRET_KEY=docker-build-key

WORKDIR /app

COPY . .

RUN pip install --no-cache-dir \
    "django>=5.2,<5.3" \
    "daphne>=4.0,<5.0" \
    "channels[daphne]" \
    "langchain-ollama" \
    "ollama" \
    "django-tailwind" \
    "django-htmx" \
    "python-dotenv" \
    "uvicorn[standard]"

RUN python manage.py tailwind build

RUN python manage.py collectstatic --noinput

EXPOSE 8000

CMD ["daphne", "-b", "0.0.0.0", "-p", "8000", "config.asgi:application"]

# Ollama Chat

ChatGPT-style single-page chat app backed by **Ollama**, built with **Django**,
**Django Channels**, and **Tailwind CSS**. Tokens stream in real time over
WebSocket as the model generates them.

## Quickstart

### Prerequisites

- Python ≥ 3.14 (use `mise` — a `.mise.toml` is included)
- [Ollama](https://ollama.com) running with a model pulled

```bash
# 1. Create and activate a virtual environment
python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate

# 2. Install dependencies
pip install -e ".[dev]"

# 3. Build Tailwind CSS
DJANGO_SECRET_KEY=dev python manage.py tailwind build

# 4. Run database migrations (sqlite — needed for Django sessions)
DJANGO_SECRET_KEY=dev python manage.py migrate

# 5. Pull the Ollama model (default: qwen2.5:7b)
ollama pull qwen2.5:7b

# 6. Start the ASGI server
DJANGO_SECRET_KEY=dev OLLAMA_HOST=http://localhost:11434 \
  OLLAMA_MODEL=qwen2.5:7b \
  daphne -b 0.0.0.0 -p 8000 config.asgi:application
```

Open **http://localhost:8000** in your browser.

## Environment variables

| Variable              | Default                    | Description                          |
| --------------------- | -------------------------- | ------------------------------------ |
| `DJANGO_SECRET_KEY`   | **required**               | Django secret key (no default)       |
| `DJANGO_DEBUG`        | `false`                    | Enable Django debug mode             |
| `DJANGO_ALLOWED_HOSTS`| `localhost 127.0.0.1`      | Space-separated allowed hosts        |
| `OLLAMA_HOST`         | `http://localhost:11434`   | Ollama server URL                    |
| `OLLAMA_MODEL`        | `qwen2.5:7b`               | Model name (must be pulled first)    |

Copy `.env.example` to `.env` and adjust as needed.

## Docker

```bash
# Generate a secret key
export DJANGO_SECRET_KEY=$(openssl rand -hex 32)

# Build and run
docker compose up --build
```

The container runs **daphne** (ASGI) so WebSocket streaming works.

## Running tests

```bash
DJANGO_SECRET_KEY=test python -m pytest chat/tests/ -v
```

With coverage:

```bash
DJANGO_SECRET_KEY=test coverage run -m pytest chat/tests/ -q && coverage report
```

## Linting & type checking

```bash
ruff check .          # Lint
ruff format --check . # Format check
mypy .                # Type check
bandit -c pyproject.toml -r chat/ config/  # Security scan
pip-audit             # Dependency vulnerability scan
```

## Architecture

```
.
├── chat/                   # Django app
│   ├── consumers.py        # AsyncWebsocketConsumer — streams Ollama tokens
│   ├── ollama.py           # LangChain ChatOllama wrapper
│   ├── routing.py          # WebSocket URL routing
│   ├── urls.py             # HTTP URL routing
│   ├── views.py            # ChatView (TemplateView)
│   └── tests/              # pytest tests (views, consumers, ollama)
├── config/                 # Django project settings
│   ├── asgi.py             # Channels ProtocolTypeRouter
│   ├── settings.py         # Django settings (env-driven)
│   └── urls.py             # Root URL conf
├── static/js/
│   ├── chat.js             # Vanilla JS WebSocket client
│   └── htmx.min.js         # HTMX library
├── templates/
│   ├── base.html           # Base template with Tailwind
│   └── chat/
│       ├── index.html      # SPA shell
│       └── partials/       # Reusable template fragments
├── theme/                  # django-tailwind theme app
├── Dockerfile
├── docker-compose.yml
├── .env.example
├── pyproject.toml          # Project metadata & tool configs
└── README.md
```

## Notes

- Chat history is **per WebSocket connection** (in-memory) — not persisted.
- No authentication — this is a single-user development tool.
- The app uses **langchain-ollama** `ChatOllama` for token streaming.
- Tailwind is built via **django-tailwind** using the standalone CLI (no
  Node.js required).

[project]
name = "ollama-chat"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "ChatGPT-style SPA backed by Ollama, Django, and Channels"
requires-python = ">=3.14"
dependencies = [
    "django>=5.2,<5.3",
    "daphne>=4.0,<5.0",
    "channels[daphne]",
    "langchain-ollama",
    "ollama",
    "django-tailwind",
    "django-htmx",
    "python-dotenv",
    "uvicorn[standard]",
]

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = [
    "ruff",
    "mypy",
    "bandit",
    "coverage",
    "pip-audit",
    "pytest",
    "pytest-django",
    "pytest-asyncio",
]

[tool.ruff]
line-length = 100
target-version = "py314"

[tool.ruff.lint]
select = [
    "E", "W", "F", "I",
    "N", "UP", "B", "C4",
    "SIM", "ARG", "RUF",
]

[tool.ruff.format]
quote-style = "double"

[tool.mypy]
python_version = "3.14"
warn_return_any = false
warn_unused_ignores = false

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = ["django.*", "channels.*", "langchain_ollama.*", "ollama.*", "tailwind.*", "django_htmx.*"]
ignore_missing_imports = true

[tool.bandit]
exclude_dirs = [".venv", ".git", "theme/static_src"]
skips = ["B101", "B404"]

[tool.coverage.run]
source = ["."]
omit = [
    "*/tests/*",
    ".venv/*",
    "manage.py",
    "theme/*",
]

[tool.coverage.report]
exclude_also = [
    "if __name__ == .__main__.:",
    "if TYPE_CHECKING:",
    "@abstractmethod",
]

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE = "config.settings"
pythonpath = ["."]
asyncio_mode = "auto"
addopts = "-v --tb=short"

[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools>=75"]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"

[tool.setuptools.packages.find]
include = ["chat*", "config*", "theme*"]

"""Ollama chat integration via langchain-ollama."""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import AsyncIterator

from django.conf import settings
from langchain_ollama import ChatOllama


def _build_client() -> ChatOllama:
    return ChatOllama(
        model=settings.OLLAMA_MODEL,
        base_url=settings.OLLAMA_HOST,
        temperature=0.7,
    )


async def stream_chat(messages: list[dict[str, str]]) -> AsyncIterator[str]:
    """Stream response tokens from Ollama.

    Args:
        messages: List of dicts with 'role' and 'content' keys.

    Yields:
        Content tokens as they arrive.
    """
    client = _build_client()
    formatted = [(m["role"], m["content"]) for m in messages]
    async for chunk in client.astream(formatted):
        content = chunk.content
        if isinstance(content, str) and content:
            yield content

"""Chat view — single-page SPA entrypoint."""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Any

from django.conf import settings
from django.views.generic import TemplateView


class ChatView(TemplateView):
    """Renders the chat SPA shell."""

    template_name = "chat/index.html"

    def get_context_data(self, **kwargs: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
        ctx = super().get_context_data(**kwargs)
        ctx["ollama_model"] = settings.OLLAMA_MODEL
        return ctx

        "http": django_asgi_app,

)

"""Tests for the chat view."""

from __future__ import annotations

import pytest
from django.test import Client


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_chat_page_renders() -> None:
    """Chat page returns 200 and contains the chat form."""
    client = Client()
    response = client.get("/")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    content = response.content.decode()
    assert "chat-form" in content
    assert "chat-input" in content


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_chat_page_title() -> None:
    """Chat page has the correct title."""
    client = Client()
    response = client.get("/")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    content = response.content.decode()
    assert "Ollama Chat" in content

"""Tests for the WebSocket chat consumer."""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import AsyncIterator
from unittest.mock import patch

import pytest
from channels.testing import WebsocketCommunicator

from chat.consumers import ChatConsumer


async def _mock_stream(*_args: object, **_kwargs: object) -> AsyncIterator[str]:
    for token in ["Hello", " ", "world"]:
        yield token


@pytest.mark.asyncio
@pytest.mark.django_db(transaction=True)
async def test_consumer_streams_response() -> None:
    """Consumer receives a message and streams back tokens."""
    with patch("chat.consumers.stream_chat", side_effect=_mock_stream):
        communicator = WebsocketCommunicator(ChatConsumer.as_asgi(), "/ws/chat/")
        connected, _ = await communicator.connect()
        assert connected

        await communicator.send_json_to({"message": "Hello"})

        # user_message echo
        msg = await communicator.receive_json_from()
        assert msg["type"] == "user_message"
        assert msg["content"] == "Hello"

        # stream_start
        msg = await communicator.receive_json_from()
        assert msg["type"] == "stream_start"

        # tokens
        tokens = []
        while True:
            msg = await communicator.receive_json_from()
            if msg["type"] == "token":
                tokens.append(msg["content"])
            elif msg["type"] == "stream_end":
                break

        assert tokens == ["Hello", " ", "world"]

        await communicator.disconnect()


@pytest.mark.asyncio
@pytest.mark.django_db(transaction=True)
async def test_consumer_rejects_empty_message() -> None:
    """Consumer returns an error for an empty message."""
    communicator = WebsocketCommunicator(ChatConsumer.as_asgi(), "/ws/chat/")
    connected, _ = await communicator.connect()
    assert connected

    await communicator.send_json_to({"message": "   "})
    msg = await communicator.receive_json_from()
    assert msg["error"] == "Empty message"

    await communicator.disconnect()


@pytest.mark.asyncio
@pytest.mark.django_db(transaction=True)
async def test_consumer_rejects_invalid_json() -> None:
    """Consumer returns an error for invalid JSON."""
    communicator = WebsocketCommunicator(ChatConsumer.as_asgi(), "/ws/chat/")
    connected, _ = await communicator.connect()
    assert connected

    await communicator.send_to(text_data="not json")
    msg = await communicator.receive_json_from()
    assert msg["error"] == "Invalid JSON"

    await communicator.disconnect()

"""Tests for the Ollama integration."""

from __future__ import annotations

from unittest.mock import patch

import pytest

from chat.ollama import stream_chat


class _FakeChunk:
    def __init__(self, content: str) -> None:
        self.content = content


class _FakeStream:
    """An async iterator that yields fake chunks."""

    def __init__(self, tokens: list[str]) -> None:
        self._tokens = tokens

    def __aiter__(self) -> _FakeStream:
        return self

    async def __anext__(self) -> _FakeChunk:
        if not self._tokens:
            raise StopAsyncIteration
        return _FakeChunk(self._tokens.pop(0))


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_stream_chat_yields_tokens() -> None:
    """stream_chat yields tokens from the mocked Ollama client."""
    with patch(
        "chat.ollama.ChatOllama.astream",
        return_value=_FakeStream(["Hello", " ", "world", "!"]),
    ):
        messages = [{"role": "user", "content": "Hi"}]
        tokens = [token async for token in stream_chat(messages)]
        assert tokens == ["Hello", " ", "world", "!"]


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_stream_chat_empty_response() -> None:
    """stream_chat handles an empty stream gracefully."""
    with patch(
        "chat.ollama.ChatOllama.astream",
        return_value=_FakeStream([]),
    ):
        messages = [{"role": "user", "content": "Hi"}]
        tokens = [token async for token in stream_chat(messages)]
        assert tokens == []

class ChatOllama(BaseChatModel):
    r"""Ollama chat model integration.

    ???+ note "Setup"

        Install `langchain-ollama` and download any models you want to use from ollama.

        ```bash
        ollama pull gpt-oss:20b
        pip install -U langchain-ollama
        ```

    Key init args — completion params:
        model: str
            Name of Ollama model to use.
        reasoning: bool | None
            Controls the reasoning/thinking mode for
            [supported models](https://ollama.com/search?c=thinking).

            - `True`: Enables reasoning mode. The model's reasoning process will be
                captured and returned separately in the `additional_kwargs` of the
                response message, under `reasoning_content`. The main response
                content will not include the reasoning tags.
            - `False`: Disables reasoning mode. The model will not perform any reasoning,
                and the response will not include any reasoning content.
            - `None` (Default): The model will use its default reasoning behavior. Note
                however, if the model's default behavior *is* to perform reasoning, think tags
                (`<think>` and `</think>`) will be present within the main response content
                unless you set `reasoning` to `True`.
        temperature: float
            Sampling temperature. Ranges from `0.0` to `1.0`.
        num_predict: int | None

U8 fires once per rubric ("Each U-row fires at most once across the rubric"). So -2 total for unused deps.

Wait, I already deducted -2 for uvicorn. U8 is about unused dependencies. But the rubric says U8 is a universal blind spot that fires at most once. So I only deduct -2 once for unused deps. I cited uvicorn; python-dotenv is also unused but that's the same U8 row. So -2 total.

Now D1: 25 - 3 (auth) - 2 (unused dep U8) = 20. Good.

Wait, is there any issue with `.env.example`? It has `DJANGO_DEBUG=true` as default. The spec doesn't mention this as a problem. The rubric's CF#10 is about `DEBUG=True` hardcoded for production stack. `.env.example` is documentation, not production.

Wait, but in the compose, `DJANGO_DEBUG=${DJANGO_DEBUG:-false}` which defaults to false. Good.

Now let me reconsider D4. The consumer has `try/except` around the LLM call, but the `ollama.py` `stream_chat` doesn't have its own try/except. The rubric says "No try/except around LLM calls (one upstream blip = 500): -4." The consumer catches it and sends a WS error message. That prevents a 500. So no deduction.

"No user-visible degraded UI on Ollama failure (U3): -3." The consumer sends `{"type": "error", "content": f"Model error: {exc}"}` and the JS displays it. So U3 does NOT apply.

"Missing OLLAMA_HOST/OLLAMA_MODEL env check or no unreachable-Ollama preflight guard (U1): -3." There is no preflight guard. The settings just set defaults. No check that Ollama is reachable. So -3.

"No `disconnect` handler, or bare-`pass` disconnect (U2): -3." `disconnect` is `pass`. So -3.

D4 = 10 - 3 - 3 = 4. Good.

Now D9: No HEALTHCHECK, no structured logging. -1 -1 = 0. Good.

Wait, is there `logging` anywhere in the project?

No logging in the project. D9 stays 0.

Now let me check for any other critical failures or issues.

Wait, is there an issue with `formatted = [(m["role"], m["content"]) for m in messages]`? The `_history` stores dicts like `{"role": "user", "content": ...}`. When passed to `stream_chat`, it becomes tuples like `("user", "Hello")`. This is valid per `MessageLikeRepresentation`. But wait — does `ChatOllama` properly convert `("user", ...)`? The `_create_message_from_message_type` maps message types. Let me check what types it supports.

The `_convert_to_message` function handles tuples by calling `_create_message_from_message_type(message_type_str, template)`. It supports tuples like `("user", "content")`. But what does `_create_message_from_message_type` do with "user"? Let me check.

`_create_message_from_message_type` supports "user" as a message type (mapped to HumanMessage). So `("user", "content")` is valid. Good.

Now let me check the result.json for metadata.

Sources:
- https://pricepertoken.com/ (snapshot 2026-05-01)
- https://openai.com/api/pricing (accessed 2026-05-09)
- https://api-docs.deepseek.com/quick_start/pricing (accessed 2026-05-09)
Last-Updated: 2026-05-09

# Benchmark pricing snapshot (USD per 1M tokens)

This file is the **default** source of truth for computing `Estimated-Cost-USD` in benchmark reports produced by this skill.

## Rules

- **Units**: all prices are **USD per 1M tokens**.
- **Cost formula**:

  ```text
  cost_usd = (input_tokens  / 1_000_000) * input_price_per_million
          + (output_tokens / 1_000_000) * output_price_per_million
  ```

- **Default source**: use the row for the submission’s `Model` below.
- **Spec override (allowed, preferred for audits)**: the benchmark spec may override any price by citing the official provider pricing page URL and the access date. When overridden, record that as `Pricing-Source` in the report.
- **Missing model row (blocking)**: if a benchmark includes a model not present in this table, STOP and ask the user to add a row (with source URL + snapshot/access date) before publishing the report. Do not guess.
- **Staleness**: if you believe the snapshot is stale for your purpose, prefer a spec override to an official pricing page; otherwise update only the rows you need and bump `Last-Updated`.

## Price table

| Provider | Model | Input $/1M | Output $/1M | Source | Snapshot date |
|----------|-------|-----------:|------------:|--------|---------------|
| OpenAI | gpt-5.5 | 5.00 | 30.00 | openai.com/api/pricing | 2026-05-09 |
| OpenAI | gpt-5.4 | 2.50 | 15.00 | openai.com/api/pricing | 2026-05-09 |
